title: tephroseris subscaposa
intro: Tephroseris subscaposa, also known as the mountain everlasting, is a flowering plant species in the daisy family that grows in the high-altitude regions of the Himalayas. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, and is believed to have the ability to reduce fever and alleviate respiratory issues. The health benefits of Tephroseris subscaposa include its potential to help with bronchitis, coughs, and other respiratory problems, as well as its use as an expectorant to aid in the removal of mucus. Herbal preparations of Tephroseris subscaposa include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
